body.title: [sprint] Populate edit-format conformance registry from accumulated corpus replay
body.description:
**Problem:** the edit-format conformance machinery (T-aider-port-editformat-conformance) shipped with the registry EMPTY — at build time `format` had only just been stamped onto editor_verdict corpus records and both the block and whole-file editors were brand new, so there were zero per-format runs to compute a rate from. select_edit_format therefore returns block for every model and the runtime ladder does all the real work. This ticket populates the registry once real data exists.

**Proposed shape:** an offline replay step that reads the accumulated editor_verdict corpus records (kind=editor_verdict, now carrying model+format+applied), runs edit_format.compute_conformance over them, and writes the resulting per-(model,format) rates into ModelSpec.edit_format_conformance in models_registry. Then select_edit_format begins choosing whole-file up-front for models that conform poorly to block (warm lookup, zero inference).

**Gate condition:** wait until the block editor has run enough real tickets under BOTH dialects to yield a meaningful sample (rule of thumb: >=20 format-stamped editor_verdict records spanning >=2 models, at least one with both formats attempted). Until then the registry stays empty and the ladder suffices.

**Affected files:** models_registry (write the field), a small replay/populate script (devlab tooling); reads io_corpus.
**Design rules:** conformance computed OFFLINE from replay (zero-inference routing data), exactly like tier routing; NO SQLITE.
**Scope boundary:** the replay->registry population + verifying select_edit_format then moves a poorly-block-conforming model to whole-file. NOT new mechanism (all built in P8).
**Test plan:** given accumulated records, compute_conformance populates the registry and select_edit_format returns whole-file for a model whose block-conformance is measurably worse; a model with no data still returns block.
**Completion criteria:** registry populated from real replay; at least one model's start-format moves off block by warm lookup; no inference in the selection path.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
